close_reason: Design decision: ?field= rejected at parse time is correct
description:
secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs:94-101 explicitly rejects the `?field=` URI parameter at construction time with a clear error message that names the limitation and points the user at backends that DO support field extraction (aws-sm). This is correct: silently ignoring `?field=` would let a user write `secretx:keyring:my-app/secret?field=token` expecting JSON-field extraction and get back the entire opaque value with no warning.

This decision should be defended against any 'simplification' PR that suggests dropping the check. The contrast is the silent-fallback path that other dispatchers might take (return raw bytes); the explicit rejection is the user-protective choice.

Tested at lib.rs:259-273, with the test asserting both the error variant AND a substring of the explanatory message.
title: Decision: ?field= rejected at parse time with explicit error
